paper_title: Diatoms as indicators of water quality in the Jukskei-Crocodile river system in 1956 and 1957, a re-analysis of diatom count data generated by BJ Cholnoky

methods.description: This method involves converting historical diatom community analysis sheets into a digital format for further analysis. The data is first entered into spreadsheets and then into the OMNIDIA database, which is designed for managing diatom samples and calculating diatom indices.

steps.description: Enter data into spreadsheets as the first step of electronic capture.
steps.searchable_summary: input diatom data into spreadsheets for digital capture

steps.description: Transfer data from spreadsheets to the OMNIDIA database.
steps.searchable_summary: transfer spreadsheet data to OMNIDIA database

steps.description: Directly enter data into OMNIDIA if proficient, bypassing spreadsheets.
steps.searchable_summary: directly input diatom data into OMNIDIA

methods.method_name: Species Name Validation and Acronym Identification
methods.description: This method addresses the challenges of entering historical diatom data using species acronyms. It involves validating species names and identifying the correct acronyms for data entry.

steps.description: Check the validity of species names in OMNIDIA or literature resources.
steps.searchable_summary: validate species names using OMNIDIA or literature

steps.description: Identify the relevant acronym for data entry using OMNIDIA's list or search function.

methods.description: Once the data is entered into the OMNIDIA database, this method involves calculating various diatom indices to analyze the diatom community data.

steps.description: Calculate diatom indices using the OMNIDIA database.
steps.searchable_summary: compute diatom indices from OMNIDIA database
